steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says cacao's structure 'is no longer identical' to its wild ancestor, then signals 'Maize (corn) also ______.' The word 'also' signals that maize does something parallel to cacao: it differs from its wild ancestor. The next two sentences confirm this — the ancestor had few small kernels, but selective breeding produced many large ones.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — varies from: means 'differs from.' Domesticated maize differs structurally from its wild ancestor. ✓
  • Choice B — helps with: maize does not assist its ancestor in any way described in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — reacts to: implies a response to a stimulus; nothing in the passage suggests maize reacts to its ancestor. ✗
  • Choice D — argues with: nonsensical — a plant cannot argue. ✗
That is the correct answer.
